Name: StratoSecure Security Platform
Author: Soluciones Etech Corp
Description: Hybrid ASPM solution correlating code-level SAST/SCA findings with Microsoft Defender for Cloud runtime evidence. Delivers regulatory compliance evidence (PCI-DSS 4.0, CNBV, SBP) and PR quality gates for banking security teams.

AnalyticRules.DisplayName: SAST Auth Finding Correlated with Brute Force
AnalyticRules.Description: Detects applications with open SAST authentication findings that are simultaneously experiencing brute force login attempts in Azure AD.
AnalyticRules.Name: S1_SAST_Auth_BruteForce

AnalyticRules.DisplayName: Secret Exposure with Anomalous SPN Sign-In
AnalyticRules.Description: Detects applications with open hardcoded-secret findings (CWE-798, CWE-259, CWE-321, CWE-522) that have associated service principal sign-in anomalies.
AnalyticRules.Name: S2_Secret_AnomalousCredential

AnalyticRules.DisplayName: BOLA Finding with API Traffic Anomaly
AnalyticRules.Description: Detects applications with open BOLA/IDOR findings (OWASP API1, API3, API5) that also show anomalous 4xx error traffic in Azure API Management.
AnalyticRules.Name: S3_BOLA_TrafficAnomaly

AnalyticRules.DisplayName: Critical Finding Overdue on Internet-Facing App
AnalyticRules.Description: Detects Critical and High severity findings older than 7 days that remain open on internet-facing applications.
AnalyticRules.Name: S6_CriticalFinding_Overdue

AnalyticRules.DisplayName: Unauthenticated API Endpoint with Sensitive Data
AnalyticRules.Description: Detects internet-facing API endpoints with no authentication that handle sensitive data classifications (PII, Financial, PHI, Confidential).
AnalyticRules.Name: S9_UnauthenticatedAPI_Internet

AnalyticRules.DisplayName: PCI App with Open Findings Before Audit Window
AnalyticRules.Description: Detects PCI-scoped applications with open Critical/High findings when an upcoming PCI-DSS audit is within 30 days.
AnalyticRules.Name: S11_PCI_AuditWindow

Watchlists.Description: Scheduled audit windows for PCI-DSS and regulatory compliance reviews.
Source code repositories designated as critical based on business impact and data sensitivity.
Security findings formally accepted as risk exceptions by the security team.
Applications and services classified as high-value crown jewels requiring enhanced monitoring.
Applications with internet-accessible endpoints that require heightened security scrutiny.
Applications in scope for PCI-DSS 4.0 compliance requiring audit trail evidence.
Development team members designated as security champions and finding owners.
Authorized scanning systems and IP ranges allowed to generate security findings.

Workbooks.WorkbookDescription: Executive dashboard showing AppSec Score trend, finding severity distribution, top overdue findings, and compliance coverage (PCI-DSS 4.0, CNBV, SBP).
SOC-facing workbook correlating code vulnerabilities with active Sentinel incidents, OWASP categories, and remediation status.
API endpoint risk inventory showing active/shadow/zombie endpoint status, unauthenticated internet-facing APIs, sensitive data exposure, and newly discovered endpoints.

Playbooks.PlaybookDescription: Creates a Jira ticket for each StratoSecure security finding incident.
Creates an Azure DevOps work item for tracking finding remediation.
Sends a Microsoft Teams notification for new critical security findings.
Assigns a finding owner in StratoSecure based on incident triage.
Creates a risk exception in StratoSecure for accepted risks.
Closes a Sentinel incident after finding is remediated.
Sends escalation email when findings remain unresolved past SLA.
